title: The Grandson's Gambit
briefing: Detective, a senior citizen just reported a suspicious text message from an unknown number. The sender claims to be her grandson, in legal trouble, and demanding gift cards immediately. Something doesn't add up — and the Agency needs your trained eye to crack this one.

evidence.html:
Unknown Number: +1 (555) 847-2931
Grandma it's me Tyler. I'm in serious trouble. I got arrested last night and I need your help RIGHT NOW
My lawyer says I need $2,000 in Google Play gift cards TODAY or I have to stay in jail until Monday. Please grandma I'm so scared
Please don't tell mom and dad — they'll be so upset with me. Just you and me, okay? This has to stay between us
Buy the cards at CVS and scratch off the back and text me the numbers. It's the only way they'll accept payment. Hurry grandma — I only have until 5pm today only

hotspots.label: Unknown phone number
hotspots.explanation: This number isn't saved in any contacts. Your real grandson's number would already be in your phone — you'd recognize it immediately.

hotspots.label: Urgent deadline — 'today only', '5pm'
hotspots.explanation: Scammers create artificial deadlines to stop you from thinking clearly or calling to verify. Real emergencies don't have 5pm cutoffs for gift card payments.

hotspots.label: Gift card payment request
hotspots.explanation: No legitimate lawyer, court, or bail bondsman accepts gift cards as payment. Gift cards are untraceable — that's exactly why scammers love them.

hotspots.label: Request for secrecy — 'don't tell mom and dad'
hotspots.explanation: Asking you to keep secrets from family is a classic manipulation tactic. It isolates you so no one can talk you out of sending money.

hotspots.label: Emotional pressure — 'I'm so scared'
hotspots.explanation: Scammers deliberately trigger fear and sympathy to override your good judgment. They know grandparents will move fast when they think a grandchild is in danger.

deduction_options.text: Grandparent scam — the unknown number, urgency, gift card demand, and secrecy request are the classic four markers
Legitimate emergency — the grandson really needs help immediately
Wrong number — someone is confused and texting the wrong person

learning_summary: Real emergencies don't require gift cards. Always call your grandchild directly on their known number before sending any money. Real lawyers and courts never ask for gift card payments.
